response: The award Alex Rodriguez won two of is the **Commissioner's Historic Achievement Award**. This award is presented by the Commissioner of Baseball to a group or person who has made a "major impact on the sport" of baseball. It is not an annual award, and the Commissioner presents the trophy at his discretion. The most recent recipient is Derek Jeter (2014), who was honored in the final year of his career for being "one of the most accomplished shortstops of all-time."
